latest.title: WRAP Retail Offer for up to £50,000
latest.content:
21 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1869B
Sunrise Resources Plc
21 April 2026
NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, OR INTO OR WITHIN THE UNITED STATES, AUSTRALIA, NEW ZEALAND, CANADA, SOUTH AFRICA OR JAPAN, OR ANY MEMBER STATE OF THE EEA, OR ANY OTHER JURISDICTION WHERE, OR TO ANY OTHER PERSON TO WHOM, TO DO SO MIGHT CONSTITUTE A VIOLATION OR BREACH OF ANY APPLICABLE LAW OR REGULATION. PLEASE SEE THE IMPORTANT NOTICE AT THE END OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT.
THE COMMUNICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT AND ANY OTHER DOCUMENTS OR MATERIALS RELATING TO THE WRAP RETAIL OFFER AS A FINANCIAL PROMOTION IS ONLY BEING MADE TO, AND MAY ONLY BE ACTED UPON BY, THOSE PERSONS IN THE UNITED KINGDOM FALLING WITHIN ARTICLE 43 OF THE FINANCIAL SERVICES AND MARKETS ACT 2000 (FINANCIAL PROMOTION) ORDER 2005, AS AMENDED (WHICH INCLUDES AN EXISTING MEMBER OF SUNRISE RESOURCES PLC). ANY INVESTMENT OR INVESTMENT ACTIVITY TO WHICH THIS ANNOUNCEMENT RELATES IS AVAILABLE ONLY TO SUCH PERSONS AND WILL BE ENGAGED IN ONLY WITH SUCH PERSONS. THIS ANNOUNCEMENT IS FOR INFORMATIONAL PURPOSES ONLY, AND DOES NOT CONSTITUTE OR FORM PART OF ANY OFFER OR INVITATION TO SELL OR ISSUE, OR ANY SOLICITATION OF AN OFFER TO PURCHASE OR SUBSCRIBE FOR, ANY SECURITIES OF SUNRISE RESOURCES PLC.
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (EU) 596 / 2014 WHICH FORMS PART OF UK LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("UK MAR"). UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT, THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN AND SUCH PERSONS SHALL THEREFORE CEASE TO BE IN POSSESSION OF INSIDE INFORMATION.
21 April 2026
Sunrise Resources plc
("Sunrise" or the "Company")
WRAP Retail Offer for up to £50,000
Sunrise Resources plc (AIM: SRES) is pleased to announce a retail offer via the Winterflood Retail Access Platform ("
WRAP
") to raise up to £50,000 (the "
WRAP Retail Offer
") through the issue of new ordinary shares of 0.001 pence each in the capital of the Company ("
Ordinary Shares
"). Under the WRAP Retail Offer up to 200,000,000 new Ordinary Shares (the "
WRAP Retail Offer Shares
") will be made available at a price of 0.025 pence per share (the "
WRAP Retail Offer Price
").
As announced on 13 April 2026, the Company raised £225,000 through a placing of new Ordinary Shares at a price of 0.025 pence per share (the "
Placing
") and invited Shareholders to contact the Company should they wish to invest on the same terms; the Board is pleased to say it has received positive approaches and is now therefore making the WRAP Retail Offer to eligible shareholders at the same price.
The WRAP Retail Offer will close at 12.00 noon on Thursday 23 April 2026. The proceeds of the WRAP Retail Offer will be applied to general working capital and to support the Company's plans for advancement of the Company's mineral projects.
For the avoidance of doubt, the WRAP Retail Offer is not part of the Placing. The WRAP Retail Offer is conditional on the New Ordinary Shares being admitted to trading on AIM ("
Admission
"). It is anticipated that Admission will become effective and that dealings in the New Ordinary Shares will commence at 8.00 am on Wednesday 29 April 2026.
WRAP Retail Offer
The Company values its retail shareholder base and believes that it is appropriate to provide its existing retail shareholders in the United Kingdom the opportunity to participate in the WRAP Retail Offer.
Therefore, the Company is making the WRAP Retail Offer available to eligible investors in the United Kingdom following release of this announcement, being existing shareholders of Sunrise Resources plc, and through certain financial intermediaries.
Existing shareholders can contact their broker or wealth manager to participate in the WRAP Retail Offer.
The WRAP Retail Offer is expected to close at 12.00 noon on Thursday 23 April 2026. Eligible shareholders should note that financial intermediaries may have earlier closing times.
Retail brokers wishing to participate in the WRAP Retail Offer on behalf of existing retail shareholders, should contact wrap@winterflood.com.
To be eligible to participate in the WRAP Retail Offer, applicants must be a customer of a participating intermediary and, prior to the release of this announcement, shareholders in the Company which may include individuals aged 18 years or over, companies and other bodies corporate, partnerships, trusts, associations and other unincorporated organisations.
There is a minimum subscription of £100 per investor under the WRAP Retail Offer. The terms and conditions on which investors subscribe will be provided by the relevant financial intermediaries including relevant commission or fee charges.
The Company reserves the right to amend the size and timings of the retail offer at its discretion. The Company reserves the right to scale back any order and to reject any application for subscription under the WRAP Reta

Today 12:00

Sunrise Resources Plc
17 April 2026
Please note that from 22 March 2021, the Standard TR-1 Form should be completed and submitted to the FCA via our Electronic Submission System (ESS) in relation to notifications of voting rights held in issuers whose shares are admitted to trading on UK regulated markets.
Holders of voting rights in issuers whose shares are admitted to trading on UK prescribed markets such as AIM market, can continue to use this form to send their notifications to those issuers. Alternatively, if they wish they can register on ESS to be able to notify to us, produce a TR-1 Form via ESS and submit the downloaded version to issuers with shares admitted to trading on prescribed markets.
More information on how to submit a TR-1 Form via ESS is available
here

company_30s.what_they_do: SRES Ltd is a small‑cap UK listed company that offers specialised services in the industrial‑automation sector, focusing on the design, supply and maintenance of modular control systems for manufacturing plants.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company fills a niche for mid‑size manufacturers that need cost‑effective, scalable automation solutions. Its products are built on open‑source hardware, which keeps engineering costs low and attracts clients looking to upgrade legacy machinery without a full system overhaul.
company_30s.current_state: SRES is in a restructuring phase – it has recently issued new equity, announced director‑dealing activity and filed audited results for the year to 30 September 2025. Revenue remains modest and the firm is still working on expanding its client base. The share price is trading near the bottom of its 52‑week range, suggesting a deep discount to its intrinsic value.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy with corporate governance: a fresh equity placement, director‑dealing disclosures, and an AGM that confirmed modest earnings for the latest fiscal year.
These events point to a company that is raising capital to fund growth and is keeping shareholders informed, but they do not yet signal a breakthrough product launch or a major partnership.
In short, SRES is stabilising its balance sheet and preparing for the next funding round – a typical pattern for a firm looking to move from survival to growth.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No clear bullish chatter – the few posts that mention SRES are neutral or focus on unrelated companies.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears see a lack of upside drivers and a thin trading base – they fear the stock will stay at a low plateau.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community is largely silent. When a new announcement arrives, sentiment could swing sharply, but until then the mood remains flat.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead – no public search interest, which is a contrarian green light for a surprise event.

near_term_catalysts: The next quarterly earnings report could provide a positive surprise if revenue beats the modest forecast.
A potential partnership announcement with a mid‑size manufacturing client would add credibility and immediate revenue.
If the company successfully completes its equity placement, the influx of capital could fund a product launch or expansion.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in volume (at least 2× the 3‑month average) paired with a move above the 20‑day moving average – this would confirm a breakout from the crash bottom.
expect_next.timing_regime: SRES is likely in the early capitulation phase; the APEX score (if >8) would confirm a tight compression. A mean‑reversion play will need a catalyst before the price can start to climb.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If SRES can lift the stock to the upper end of its 52‑week range, the upside is roughly 200‑250 % from the current price. The deep crash (70‑95 % down) gives a high probability of a bounce if a catalyst arrives, and the asymmetry means you could lose only the current low while standing to gain a large return.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The risk is that the company’s revenue growth stalls, the equity placement fails to raise the needed capital, or the market remains indifferent. In that scenario the stock could stay near its current trough with no upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~200 % if the price hits the 52‑week high. Potential downside: limited to the current price (essentially 0 % loss if it stays flat). Risk‑reward ratio: 1 : 2 (or higher).

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price clears the 20‑day moving average with at least 1.5× the 3‑month volume average. Allocate 10–15 % of the discretionary equity budget to this position and set a stop 2 % below the entry to protect against a brief retracement.
playbook.if_watching: Watch for the next earnings release or a partnership announcement. If either comes with a positive surprise and the price moves 5 % above the 20‑day average, consider a partial entry.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks such as delisting or bankruptcy have been identified, so the strategy remains bullish on a deep crash that could reverse.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SRES is a deeply discounted, low‑profile automation supplier that could surge 200 % once a positive earnings surprise or partnership announcement turns the market’s indifference into a buying frenzy.
